title: Why are files seemingly globbed together?
body:
If I have two files, which are definitely invalid verilog files:

a.v:
```
module a(
```

b.v:
```
input logic d,
output logic q
            );

assign q = d;

endmodule
```

Doing the following returns no errors:
```
$ iverilog -g2012 a.v b.v
```

But either of the two individually fail:
```
$ iverilog -g2012 a.v 
a.v:2: syntax error
I give up.
$ iverilog -g2012 b.v 
b.v:1: syntax error
I give up.
```

If I try to pass in the `-u` flag then instead I get an error that `default_nettype directives must appear outside module definitions.`

The reason I bring this up is because I have a file that iverilog won't parse, and running a similar command spat out tons of errors about invalid syntax on a file I knew parsed properly. I would have expected `-u` to be enabled by default with `-g2012`, but in any case it still resets the compiler directives to a point where they're no longer useful because things like the default nettype no longer exist. 

Is there a way to get the behavior of `-u` while preserving things like the default nettype? Can I parse a list of verilog files and not have the errors in an individual file corrupt the remaining ones?
